name: Respec
description: A system that lets players refund and reassign progression choices.
design_notes: Respec rules define how experimental the progression system feels. Costs, location requirements, refund percentages, and locked choices all affect whether players treat builds as commitments or flexible loadouts.
design_purpose: Let players recover from mistaken or outdated build choices.
Encourage experimentation with abilities and gear.
Support balance patches without permanently punishing existing saves.
player_fantasy: The player feels free to reshape a build as they learn the game.

parameters.name: respec_currency_cost
parameters.description: Cost required to start a respec.

parameters.name: locked_node_policy
parameters.description: Handling for quest, class, or story-locked choices.

edge_cases: Refunding a prerequisite must remove dependent abilities.
Removed abilities need cleanup for hotbars, passives, summons, and equipment requirements.
Balance patches may need free respec grants.
common_bugs: Passive stat bonuses remain after the node is refunded.
UI shows refunded points before validation and loses them on save reload.
Respec allows invalid builds that cannot be reloaded later.
balancing_notes: Cheap respec supports experimentation; expensive respec reinforces identity and long-term planning.
Punishing respec costs can discourage players from engaging with complex trees.
accessibility_notes: Provide confirmation, preview, and undo steps so players do not lose progress through accidental clicks.

implementation_notes.unity: Apply respec through a transaction that removes grants, validates dependencies, refunds resources, and writes one save-state update.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep build state separate from granted effects so refunding can recompute final stats from selected nodes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Centralize respec authority in a progression component and replay ability grants after validation.
implementation_notes.web: Represent selected nodes as IDs and recompute derived stats from scratch after every refund or purchase transaction.
